mnemonics: PLEASE DRINK COFFEE: Planning, Requirements, Design, Construction, Filling, Deployment.
keyPoints: The Planning Phase involves defining the project scope, goals, and feasibility.
The Requirements Phase focuses on gathering business needs and data sources.
The Design Phase creates the architectural blueprint for the warehouse.
The Construction Phase involves building the ETL processes and physical database structures.
The Filling Phase is where data is extracted, transformed, and loaded (ETL) into the warehouse.
The Deployment Phase includes testing, user training, and rolling the warehouse out for use.
commonMistakes: Skipping the Requirements Phase and designing based on assumptions.
Underestimating the time and complexity of the ETL (Filling) process.
Deploying the warehouse without proper user acceptance testing (UAT).
